target: 2. Two Who Can't Convey It, Two Who Can
Mio, sorry to keep you waiting.
Ah, Tokiko.
Wait, Sou-kun too?
Yo, Mio-chan! Nice night, huh!
Sorry for calling you out so suddenly.
It's fine. You came all the way to my place, after all.
But if you had just told me the location, I would have gone there.
We're the ones who called you out. It's only natural that we come to you.
But why is Sou-kun here too?
My brother eavesdropped on our phone call and followed me.
Hah!?
Eh, is that so?
I-I just happened to overhear it! There's no way I'd do something like eavesdrop!
Besides, look, it's dangerous to have Mio-chan walking around at night, right? I thought it would be safer if I was here...
You don't even worry about me, yet you say that?
Th-That's not true! I was definitely worrying about you too...
Alright, alright. Older brother, just shut up. You're making things complicated.
Yeah, what did you want to talk about?

Ah… okay.
Let's walk while we talk.
Yeah, it's fine with me.
Actually, Shinpei…
Hey, Mio!

If you have a problem, I'll listen!
So just tell me anything!
Huh? What's going on all of a sudden?
Older brother… didn't I tell you to be quiet?
S-Sorry… I just couldn't help it!
Even if you ask about my problems... I'm not really troubled by anything, you know?

You know, today Shinpei asked me.
He said he noticed something was off with you…
Did something happen?
Huh, Shinpei?
Yeah, he sent Tokiko a message.

By the way, my older brother found out because
he snooped on my messages.

I-I just happened to see it!
Snooping? That's not something I'd do!

Can something that happens twice in a day really be
just a coincidence?
(Shinpei sent a message to Tokiko…)
(Have I been acting so different lately?)

(Well, at least…
Shinpei saw it that way.)

Hey, Mio.
Is the problem about Shinpei?

Older brother!! 
Why'd you say that all of a sudden?!

I only throw straight pitches.
That's my specialty.
Stop making up excuses!
It's fine, Tokiko.
Mio…
Actually, I think Sou's pretty amazing.

No matter how hard it is to say,
you can say it when it's time.
Thanks, Mio.
Besides, it's just like Sou said.
I guess it was about Shinpei after all.

Mio, I'll say it again.
If you have something to talk about,
just tell me anything.

Tell me everything,
and you'll feel better.

Of course,
I won't tell Shinpei a thing.
Yeah… thanks.

It looks like we'll need more time.
Let's go to the beach and talk.

Right on!
Just tell me anything, Mio!
I'll definitely listen!
Yeah…

Wait a second.
Older brother, you should go over there.
W-Why?! I was going to help you out too...

Please!
Just for a little while.
Ah, okay…
I feel bad for Sou…
But this way, it's easier for you, right?
Y-Yeah…

Then let's continue.
Tell me everything you can.
Tokiko... you know that when Shinpei left for Tokyo, he and my sister parted on bad terms, right?
Yeah, more or less. They parted on bad terms and have been in a cold war ever since, right?
That's right.

Even though Shinpei came back,
my sister still said this morning,
"I'll hit him if I see him."
I didn't want to see the two of them fight, and I wanted them to make up...

When I saw them talking happily,
I felt relieved,
and I was happy.

But… why?
Even though I should be happy,
my chest feels all fluttery.
That's probably…
Yeah, I think I'm jealous of my sister.
I…

I think I…
like Shinpei.

Mio… you've finally been honest
with your own feelings.
Yeah… I guess so.
H-Hey, Sou!

Older brother…!
Did you hear that?

Sorry… I thought you were thirsty,
so I went to get a drink.
It looks like I came back at a bad time.
That's why I told you to go over there…
More importantly... Was what you just said true?
Yeah…

But Shinpei doesn't see me
as anything more than a sister!

Plus, when it's just me and him,
his attitude is completely different
from when it's just my sister and him!

So if I told Shinpei…
how I feel…

It probably wouldn't go anywhere.
He'd still be looking at my sister.

In that case…
it's probably best to just keep things
the way they are.

But then why do I feel…
jealous?

I really like both of them,
but I can't even genuinely support them…
I'm such a bad person.
That's not true at all!
I'll vouch for Mio's good character!

Shhh… your voice is too loud.
What if someone hears?
Ah… sorry.
Hehe…

But… I agree with you.
Mio's a good person.
You know, Mio. A person's heart isn't like a single picture that looks the same from every angle.
Your feeling of happiness that Shinpei and Ushio are getting along, and your feeling of sadness that he treats you and Ushio differently...
Both of them are your true, genuine feelings.

But if I have such a petty feeling,
I guess I really…
No, it's not like that.

Everyone has feelings they can't show to others.
I do, and so does my older brother.

There's definitely a lot of things
I can't tell anyone!
Having a lot of them is probably a bad thing, though.
Guh!
In most cases, people lock away inconvenient feelings deep in their hearts and pretend not to notice them...
But you're different. You faced those feelings without ignoring them... and tried to find your own answer.
I think the fact that you can do that makes you really wonderful.
Of course I think so too!

So Mio.
Believe in yourself more.

Yeah…
Thanks, Tokiko, Sou.
Honestly, talking about it made me feel much better.

I've been thinking about this all day.
That's probably why I looked so down
to Shinpei.

I think I understand Mio's feelings
pretty well.
Huh?
I'm the same. No matter how much I care, I can't move forward―― I'm carrying those kinds of feelings too.
Hey, could it be… that you have someone you like, Tokiko?
...That look on your face, you do.

Huh?!
Tokiko, is that really true?!
This is the first time I'm hearing about this! Keeping something like that from your older brother――
You're so loud. Why would I tell you?
I'd rather tell a stain on the ceiling than tell you.
That's harsh…!
Anyway, why are you still here? Could you stop eavesdropping on us?
I'm not eavesdropping! Why do I have to leave after hearing this much of the conversation?!
…I mean, hey, Tokiko?
Don't tell me, the person you like is... not Shinpei, is it?
…Huh?
Ah... well, s-sure, Shinpei is... a good guy, but!
Don't just assume things. It's not Shinpei.
I-I-Is that so?!
Then, who is it that Tokiko likes?
For now, it's still a secret from everyone.
Ah~, geez! The conversation got derailed because of you.
It's my fault?!
What I wanted to say is that I can understand how you feel, Mio.
I know all too well how painful it is to keep holding onto those feelings without being able to take a step forward.
But while I can share your feelings, honestly... I can't give you a single piece of good advice.
I see… Thank you, Tokiko.
Just knowing that Tokiko understands my feelings makes me happy.
Yeah, me too. But I'm sorry I couldn't be of more help...

No, it's fine.
Don't worry about it.

Both of you, look up!
There's nothing to be sad about!
Older brother?
Watch closely! I'm gonna show you the answer right now!
…Answer?
Hey, Mio.
…No, Mio!!
Huh?!

It might not be the right time to say this.
I actually planned to say it during the summer festival.
But if the two of you are worrying about something like this…
As an older brother, and a reliable elder, I have to set an example here!
W-Wait, don't tell me...

Listen carefully, Mio!
I…
I like you! I've liked you for a long, long time...!
Eh… um…

Older brother.
Did you forget that you were rejected before?

No, I remember well!
I've been rejected twice already!

But I don't care about that!
What's important is now!
So let me say it again now!
Mio, I can't give up on you! I want you to only look at me!
B-But... that's...
I clearly heard how you feel with my own ears just now.
Even so, confessing like this might be the height of foolishness.
But I don't want to give up so easily!
Even if I stop, hesitate, and regret not doing anything, the future won't change at all.
That's why I plan to say it again and again.
Above all else, if I don't do this, my feelings for you won't settle down!
So with that said, Mio! Listen closely one more time!
Mio, I like you! I've liked you for a long, long time!
And I will from now on too! I'll always love you!
Sou...
You're an idiot, really.
But it seems this idiot has kept thinking about you in his own idiotic way.
Yeah…
Sou. It reached me. Those words just now... they really hit me.
Huh!? T-Then...!
But, um… I'm sorry.
It really didn't work~~!!
Obviously.
But... today I got a lot of courage from you, Sou.
I'm going to try moving forward like you did, Sou!
Then…
Yeah, I'll try to tell Shinpei my honest feelings.
Mio...
I see, that's good. Seems I was able to set a good example in my own way.
Sou... thank you so much.
I thought you were just being a nuisance, but I guess you can be useful sometimes.
Right? So you can rely on me more too, you know.
...Hehe, I'll think about it.
Well, I should be heading home soon.
Ah, yeah! Sorry for talking for so long.
Older brother, hurry up and wipe your tears. You're making Mio worry.
*Sniff*... Y-Yeah.
Oh, right. Should I walk you home?
No, it's fine for today. I want to think things over on the way back.
I see… Yeah, I understand.

Bye, Mio.
Be careful on your way back.
Yeah!
(Even if I know it won't go well, there's meaning in moving forward)
(If that's the case, I want to gather the courage to tell Shinpei how I feel)
(That's way better than just thinking about the results and looking gloomy!)
